relationship.description: An engineer can have multiple status records over time
A machine can have many cost entries reflecting price changes
One engineer may be assigned to many machines (historical and current)
One machine may be serviced by many engineers over its lifecycle

metadata.improvements_made: Implemented the suggested UNIQUE constraint on (engineer_id, machine_id, start_date) in ENGINEER_MACHINE to prevent overlapping assignments. Added a CHECK constraint on ENGINEER_STATUS.status to restrict values to a predefined list (e.g., 'Active', 'Inactive', 'On Leave'). Recommended and documented indexes on foreign‑key columns (engineer_id, machine_id) for performance. Clarified that a NULL end_date in ENGINEER_MACHINE denotes a current assignment. Considered but retained separate audit tables (ENGINEER_STATUS, MACHINE_COST_HISTORY) for clear temporal tracking, noting they could be abstracted to a generic AUDIT_HISTORY if future expansion requires.
